questions.question: संयुक्त-व्यञ्जनं किं भवति?
questions.themeWrapper: O brave seeker of Sanskrit knowledge! Answer this challenge: When two or more consonants (व्यञ्जन) combine together, what is formed?

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct वर्ण-वियोगः (letter analysis) of the word 'क्रीडा'?
questions.themeWrapper: Warrior of words, decode the syllable-shield! Identify the correct breakdown of 'क्रीडा' as taught in the chapter:

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word 'जाह्नवी' as given in the chapter's vocabulary (शब्दार्थाः)?
questions.themeWrapper: Quest champion, consult the sacred word-treasury of Deepakam! What does 'जाह्नवी' mean?
options: Rishi Jahnu's daughter — the Ganga river
The group of seven great sages
questions.sampleAnswer: Rishi Jahnu's daughter — the Ganga river (जह्नु ऋषेः पुत्री – गङ्गा)

questions.question: Do वर्ण-संयोगः for the following: Write the complete word formed from these letters — म् + अ + ञ् + ज् + उ + ल् + अ + ः
questions.themeWrapper: Hero of Sanskrit, forge these letter-fragments into a mighty word! Combine the given वर्णाः and write the resulting शब्दः along with its meaning from the chapter.
questions.sampleAnswer: The combined word is 'मञ्जुलः', which means lovely or charming (सुन्दरः / रमणीयः).

questions.question: Perform वर्ण-वियोगः (letter analysis) of the word 'ब्रह्मचारी' and write each component वर्णः separately.
questions.themeWrapper: O fearless explorer of Deepakam! Unsheathe your analytical sword and break down the word 'ब्रह्मचारी' into its individual वर्णाः, as shown by the examples in Chapter 2.
questions.sampleAnswer: ब्रह्मचारी = ब् + र् + अ + ह् + म् + अ + च् + आ + र् + ई — This word contains the conjunct consonant 'ह्म' (ह् + म्) as well as 'ब्र' (ब् + र्).

questions.question: Chapter 2 of Deepakam focuses on संयुक्त-व्यञ्जनानि (conjunct consonants). (a) Explain in your own words how conjunct consonants are formed. (b) Give any five examples of conjunct consonants from the chapter along with their वर्ण-वियोगः. (c) Write the meanings (in Hindi or English) of any three words from the chapter's शब्दार्थाः section that contain conjunct consonants.
questions.themeWrapper: Grand Quest Champion, this is your ultimate trial! Demonstrate your mastery of संयुक्त-व्यञ्जनानि — the combined consonant warriors of Sanskrit. Answer all three parts to claim full glory!
questions.sampleAnswer: (a) Conjunct consonants (संयुक्त-व्यञ्जनानि) are formed when two or more consonants (व्यञ्जनाः) combine together without any vowel in between. They are not new letters but a special writing style of combining individual consonants. For example, क् + ष् = क्ष, and ज् + ञ् = ज्ञ. (b) Five examples with वर्ण-वियोगः: 1. क्षा (कक्षा) = क् + ष् + आ; 2. पत्रम् = प् + अ + त् + र् + अ + म्; 3. ज्ञानम् = ज् + ञ् + आ + न् + अ + म्; 4. विद्या = व् + इ + द् + य् + आ; 5. राष्ट्रम् = र् + आ + ष् + ट् + र् + अ + म्. (c) Three word meanings: 1. वज्रम् — Indra's weapon / Diamond (इन्द्रस्य आयुधम् / हीरकम्); 2. ब्रह्म — Ultimate Reality / Supreme God (परमात्मा / परमेश्वर); 3. संयमः — Self-control (आत्मानुशासनम्).
